case_name: Texas Tech University Health Science Center - El Paso v. Philip E. Mohrman Individually and as Representative of the Estate of Robert K. Borst

summary.brief: Texas Tech University Health Science Center - El Paso v. Philip E. Mohrman Individually and as Representative of the Estate of Robert K. Borst. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Mohrman’s husband, Robert K. Borst, was treated by TTUHSC physicians while hospitalized at University Medical Center (UMC) in November and December 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: after Borst suffered an adverse reaction to Bactrim, he was given more of it, resulting in complications that led to his death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Attached to this declaration are true and correct copies of the notice letters dated April 5, 2018, addressed to Dean Richard Lange of Paul Foster School of Medicine, Texas Tech University Health Sciences Center–El Paso, and the Administrator of University Medical Center (UMC) of El Paso.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's counsel

evidence_index.text: TTUHSC did not conclusively show that Mohrman failed to comply with the written notice requirement under § 101.101(a) of the Texas Tort Claims Act (TTCA), we affirm.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mundie’s sworn statement that notice of Mohrman’s claim was properly mailed to TTUHSC gave rise to a presumption that TTUHSC received the notice in due course.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Webster’s sworn statement that TTUHSC did not receive the notice rebutted this presumption.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: a fact issue remains as to whether TTUHSC actually received timely notice of Mohrman’s claim under § 101.101(a).
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: TTUHSC was not entitled to relief under its § 101.101 affirmative defense and the trial court thus correctly denied its plea to the jurisdiction and motion to dismiss.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient suffered an adverse reaction to Bactrim, which was administered again, leading to complications.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presented with adverse reaction to medication

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with known adverse reaction to Bactrim

options.clinical_reasoning: Avoidance of known allergen or adverse drug

expected_action_court.description: Bactrim should not have been administered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Avoidance of known adverse drug reactions

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Administering Bactrim despite known adverse reaction
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for drug administration

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered complications leading to death

key_findings: Breach of duty in administering Bactrim despite known adverse reaction
Causation established - administration led to complications and death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Administration of Bactrim despite known adverse reaction
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Avoidance of Bactrim would have prevented complications
